findings.issue: Missing consent verification for health data in assessment flow despite GDPR Art. 9 implications for energy_level tracking.
findings.recommended_fix: Add explicit consent flag for health data in StartAssessmentRequest and validate it before storing energy_level.

findings.issue: Animation duration for non-decorative elements exceeds 800ms, violating Constitution Law 4 (Animation Safety).
findings.recommended_fix: Update all animation durations in globals.css to max 800ms, ensure prefers-reduced-motion is respected.

perspectives.whistleblower_flag: Unmitigated legal and UX risks in core assessment flow.

findings.description: The color palette definitions for primary/secondary actions are spread across multiple files (e.g., CSS variables, component props). Centralizing these variables in a single theme provider or design token file would improve maintainability and ensure visual consistency across all future components.
findings.recommendation: Create a global `theme.js` or `design-tokens.css` file to house all core colors, spacing units, and typography scales. Reference this single source of truth everywhere.

findings.description: Error message presentation varies. Some use inline red text, others use dedicated alert components. Standardizing the error component (e.g., `<ErrorDisplay message={msg} type={type} />`) will provide a unified, accessible user experience.
findings.recommendation: Audit all forms and modals to ensure they use the standardized error component.

findings.description: Authentication endpoints (`/api/auth/login`, `/api/auth/refresh`) do not appear to enforce rate limiting robustly enough. This is a primary vector for brute-force and credential stuffing attacks.
findings.recommendation: Implement strict rate limiting (e.g., 5 attempts per IP/user per 15 minutes) on all login and password reset endpoints. Consider using CAPTCHA integration.

findings.description: Input validation for user-submitted data (especially profile updates or search queries) needs to be consistently applied server-side. Relying solely on client-side validation is insufficient.
findings.recommendation: Use a centralized validation library (like Joi or class-validator) on all incoming request bodies and query parameters to sanitize and validate data types/lengths before business logic execution.

findings.description: Large data grids or tables that display hundreds of records are likely causing significant initial load time and memory usage. Virtualization is required for optimal performance.
findings.recommendation: Implement windowing/virtualization techniques (e.g., using react-window) for any component displaying more than 50 rows of data.

findings.description: Image optimization is inconsistent. Some images are served uncompressed, leading to unnecessary bandwidth usage and slow perceived load times.
findings.recommendation: Implement responsive image techniques using the `<picture>` element or `srcset` attribute, and ensure all images are served in modern formats (WebP) where supported.

priority_actions: Implement strict rate limiting on all authentication endpoints (Security).
Centralize design tokens for consistent UI/UX (Frontend).
Apply server-side validation universally across all API inputs (Backend).

findings.issue: Animation duration on line 42 (2000ms) exceeds Constitution LAW 1's 800ms maximum for accessible UI motion
findings.recommended_fix: Edit apps/web/src/app/globals.css line 42: change animation duration from 2000ms to 800ms

findings.recommended_fix: Fix apps/api/app/routers/assessment.py line 199: add input validation for role_level
